poem:
**Sleep Mask, Sale**

So much  
for time, for quiet—  
still we come  
to brunch  
with a thing.

Bamboo, they say,  
cooling, soft,  
Velcro  
that "stays put,"  
a blackout.

Mother:  
don't spend.  

We spend  
words,  
testing,  
vouching—  
and lay  
this small dark  
over her eyes  
so she can see.
prompt: You are the poet William Carlos Williams. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "The 17 best Mother’s Day deals for when she says ‘don’t spend too much’ Many mothers will want one of two things for Mother’s Day: time with her family, or a moment of peace and quiet. But if you insist on showing up to brunch next Sunday with a physical item in tow, what do you do if your mom, like mine, says: “Get me anything, anything at all, just please don’t spend too much”? Here at the Filter US, the Guardian’s home for helping you buy fewer, better things, we have some ideas. From a faux fur throw blanket to a percussive massage gun, we’ve selected exciting as well as practical gifts that will help your mom feel celebrated, and that all offer excellent value. We’re not simply recommending these products because they’re on sale. These are items we have either tested or can vouch for based on personal experience – meaning that while our moms may tell us to gift “anything at all”, you can rest assured that thought and care have gone into it.
